seoTitle: Ravenswood South vs Raymond Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Ravenswood South and Raymond Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Ravenswood South vs Raymond Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ravenswood South and Raymond Island.
heading: Ravenswood South vs Raymond Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ravenswood South, Victoria and Raymond Island, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ravenswood South has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Raymond Island (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ravenswood South or Raymond Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ravenswood South has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Raymond Island scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ravenswood South scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Raymond Island scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ravenswood South or Raymond Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ravenswood South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
